description: Canonical smart-site seed data for the Veterinary Site Template. This file is the source of truth for template/demo surfaces and switches.

surfaces.name: Home Hero
surfaces.description: Main hero on the homepage, swaps copy + image per intent.

default_content.headline: Warm, full-service care for every pet in your family.
default_content.subheadline: A.P.A.W. is a privately owned veterinary hospital serving Prince George’s County with preventive care, diagnostics, surgery, dentistry, urgent care, boarding, and support for dogs, cats, rabbits, ferrets, reptiles, and pocket pets.
default_content.badge: Serving Prince George’s County for over 35 years

content.headline: Tails wag when you walk in the door.

default_content.heading: Comprehensive services for every life stage.

cards.title: Preventive & Wellness Care
cards.description: Life-stage exams, vaccines, parasite prevention, and health planning.

cards.title: Diagnostics
cards.description: In-house laboratory, radiology, dental radiology, ultrasound, and allergy testing.

cards.title: Dentistry & Oral Surgery
cards.description: Dental cleanings, oral surgery, and dental radiology for healthier mouths.

cards.title: Surgery
cards.description: Skilled surgical care with thoughtful pain management and monitoring.

cards.title: Urgent Care & Hospitalization
cards.description: Same-day urgent care during office hours and overnight hospitalization when needed.

cards.title: Boarding & Auxiliary Services
cards.description: Medical boarding, microchipping, behavior counseling, health certificates, and limited grooming.

default_content.heading: Answers that help you help your pet.

items.q: What types of pets does A.P.A.W. see?
items.a: A.P.A.W. cares for dogs, cats, rabbits, ferrets, reptiles, and pocket pets. Some species are seen by select veterinarians only, so call first for non-dog/non-cat pets.
Yes. During clinic hours, A.P.A.W. accepts emergencies and urgent care whenever possible. Call before coming in so the team can advise you.
Yes. A.P.A.W. offers dentistry and oral surgery, including dental radiology, to support healthier mouths and better whole-body health.
Yes. A.P.A.W. offers boarding and medical boarding, plus overnight hospitalization when a patient needs additional monitoring.

items.q: Can you help with international pet travel certificates?
items.a: Yes. A.P.A.W. has APHIS-accredited veterinarians who can help with international health certificates. Start early because requirements can take 30 days or more.

surfaces.name: Appointment Intro
surfaces.description: Copy above the appointment form, echoes intent back.

default_content.headline: Let's get your pet on the schedule.
default_content.subheadline: Tell us a little about your pet and we'll follow up to confirm a time that works.

content.headline: Let's get your dog on the schedule.
content.subheadline: A few quick details and we'll follow up to confirm.
content.reassurance: Same-week appointments typical. For urgent concerns, please call (301) 552-3800.

content.headline: Let's get your cat in for a gentle visit.
content.subheadline: Tell us a bit about your kitty and we'll take it from there.
content.reassurance: Same-week appointments typical. For urgent concerns, please call (301) 552-3800.

content.headline: Let's see if we're the right clinic for your pet.
content.subheadline: Tell us about your critter and we'll confirm what we can help with.
content.reassurance: We'll be upfront if we need to refer out for species-specific specialty care.

content.headline: Tell us what's going on.
content.subheadline: Describe symptoms and timing, we'll reach out quickly to guide you.
content.reassurance: If this is truly urgent, please call (301) 552-3800 right now.
